prompt:
Scene:
Generate a third square continuation for Banksy Street Satire, again preserving the reference framing and composition map while raising the social or narrative stakes. The image should feel like the same visual system encountering a denser human, cultural, or environmental layer, with crisp depth and disciplined lighting.

Subject:
Keep the reference subject structure stable, but add a more complex interaction around it: crowd logic, surrounding artifacts, or situational tension that still leaves the original motif unmistakable. The viewer should recognize the lineage immediately before discovering the new narrative charge.

Important details:
Honor the same primary contour language from image 01. Use the theme's hardest promise, the theme needs concept, composition, and finish to arrive in one image, as the guide. Lean on reasoning-composition, style-transfer so the scene stays readable even with added complexity. the image should read as difficult, coherent, and immediately persuasive

Use case:
Banksy Street Satire series study 3/8, continuity variation with denser narrative pressure.

Constraints:
No readable text, no watermark, no logos, preserve exact 1:1 aspect ratio, preserve the same dominant subject identity, avoid chaotic crowding, avoid posterized mush, avoid flat empty walls unless they serve the composition, and avoid breaking the reference continuity.
title: Social Shift
description: A reference-dependent variation that keeps the same core composition but adds stronger narrative or public-world pressure.

generation.runner_notes: Uses image 01 as the primary reference for narrative continuity.
